decision: 'New issue' = created_at within lookback_days; paginate created-desc and early-stop past the cutoff
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC says 'issue mới mở' (newly opened); created_at is the literal match and enables efficient early-stop pagination

decision: Exclude any item carrying a pull_request field; collect issues only
reasoning: GitHub /issues returns PRs too; including them pollutes the digest and contradicts V1 scope

decision: Bound pagination by max_items_per_repo (default 100) OR lookback cutoff, whichever first; follow Link rel=next
reasoning: Hot repos can open hundreds of issues/week; unbounded fetch burns the 5000/hr budget and produces an unreadable digest

decision: Collector is pure I/O — returns list[RawItem], never reads/writes StateStore; pipeline owns record/delta
reasoning: Honors the ports.py boundary + idempotency design; keeps S2 mockable in isolation; delta logic centralized for V2
rejected: Collector reads StateStore to skip seen

decision: Per-repo 404/410 -> warn+skip+continue; 401/403 auth -> fail fast
reasoning: On a watchlist one bad repo must not abort the run, but a broken token affects all repos and must surface clearly; matches 4xx-permanent error policy

decision: Fetch state=all sorted created desc
reasoning: An issue opened then closed inside the window is still 'new'; created-desc enables early-stop

decision: item_id = str(issue number); body null -> ''; created_at kept as raw ISO string
reasoning: Matches models.RawItem shape exported by project-foundation

decision: Bounded exponential backoff, max 3 retries on 429/5xx; lookback cutoff computed in UTC
reasoning: Sensible default matching conventions.md retryable policy; UTC avoids drift vs GitHub Z timestamps; architect may refine numbers at S3

decision: GITHUB_TOKEN never logged/echoed; redact Authorization if a request is ever logged
reasoning: STRIDE High info-disclosure threat; token is the only secret in this component
rejected: log request on error for debugging

decision: Collector re-validates repo against ^[\w.-]+/[\w.-]+$ before any request
reasoning: repo builds the request path (SSRF-shaped); defense-in-depth over S1 config validation

decision: GITHUB_TOKEN sent via Authorization header but never logged/echoed/returned; redact on any request logging
reasoning: STRIDE T-I1 High; token is the only secret; matches security.md zero-tolerance on tokens-in-logs
rejected: log request on error for debugging

decision: Null/missing GitHub fields coerce to safe defaults (body null -> ''); never assume shape
reasoning: STRIDE T-T1 High; conventions.md treat-all-response-data-untrusted

decision: On hitting max_items_per_repo cap, emit info-level truncation log
reasoning: Contentious point from S1 handoff — truncated repo digest must not be silent (EC-016)

decision: 403 + X-RateLimit-Remaining:0 = backoff, not permanent auth failure; only other 401/403 fail fast
reasoning: 403 ambiguity flagged in S1 handoff §4; secondary rate limit must not abort run

decision: Retry policy = bounded exponential backoff, default max 3, then surface error
reasoning: ASSUMED in S1 (not user-confirmed); sensible default per conventions.md retryable policy; architect/S3 may refine exact numbers

decision: item_id = str(issue number); created_at kept as raw ISO string
reasoning: Matches models.RawItem shape exported by project-foundation; downstream cache key uses item_id

decision: Collector re-validates repo against ^[\w.-]+/[\w.-]+$ before any request
reasoning: STRIDE T-T2; defense-in-depth over S1 config validation for SSRF-shaped path

decision: state=all and sort=created&direction=desc
reasoning: created-desc enables early-stop pagination; state=all keeps opened-then-closed issues in scope

decision: Collector implements ports.GitHubClient Protocol fetch_items(repo, lookback_days) -> list[RawItem]
reasoning: Contract already exported by project-foundation; pure I/O boundary

decision: Inject collector tunables via a CollectorConfig (+ nested RetryPolicy) through GitHubCollector.__init__; leave the port signature and S1 Config dataclass untouched
reasoning: BR-2-013/014 need config-driven tunables but fetch_items(repo,lookback_days) carries no config and S1 Config is a frozen contract; DI localizes tunables to the adapter and keeps INT-2-001 intact
rejected: pass tunables as fetch_items kwargs (breaks the Protocol)

decision: One RetryPolicy object + a single _request_with_retry wrapper around ALL GETs; wait=min(base*mult**attempt + U(0,jitter), ceiling), Retry-After takes precedence (capped by ceiling); inject sleep
reasoning: BR-2-014/AC-2-026 require retry behind one config object, not scattered constants; single wrapper is the only httpx caller and is unit-testable

decision: Single _classify(response) -> {OK,RETRY,SKIP_REPO,FAIL_FAST}; 403 splits on X-RateLimit-Remaining:0 (RETRY) vs other 401/403 (FAIL_FAST); 404/410 SKIP_REPO; 429/5xx RETRY
reasoning: 403 ambiguity (AC-2-008 vs AC-2-020) and per-repo isolation vs fail-fast must be deterministic and directly testable; mirrors the Error/Outcome table
rejected: branch inline as statuses are seen

decision: Token isolation: auth set on httpx client at construction; token never stored in CollectorConfig/repr; error messages = status+repo+static reason only; dedicated redaction test
reasoning: T-I1 High (AC-2-009) — any path that stringifies request/headers leaks the token; structural prevention + explicit test

decision: Mock httpx with httpx.MockTransport (no new dependency); adapter accepts an optional injected client/transport
reasoning: All tests must mock GitHub (stack rule) and respx is not a declared dep; MockTransport is first-class httpx with full status/header/Link control

decision: Promote config._REPO_RE to a public osspulse.config.REPO_PATTERN constant and reuse it in the Collector; do not redefine the regex
reasoning: AC-2-014/BR-2-011 require repo re-validation; two drifting regexes are a latent bug — single source of truth
rejected: re-declare a separate regex in the Collector

decision: No openapi.yaml for this change (R5 deviation); document the single outbound GitHub call in design §API Design + §Error Mapping instead
reasoning: OSS Pulse exposes no inbound HTTP API (conventions.md: N/A); the only HTTP is GitHub's own outbound contract we don't own — an openapi.yaml would document a third-party API
rejected: author an openapi.yaml describing GitHub's /issues endpoint

decision: Authorization header uses 'Bearer <token>'; recommend a fine-grained read-only PAT for the operator
reasoning: Bearer works for both classic and fine-grained PATs and is required for fine-grained; read-only fine-grained PAT matches least-privilege (STRIDE T-E1 / AC-2-013). Confirmed with user.

decision: Tunables run on CollectorConfig defaults in V1; reading them from TOML is deferred (out of scope), confirmed by user
reasoning: proposal locks 'does not change the required config contract from S1'; wiring TOML for tunables would expand scope and require an S2 return

decision: CollectorConfig.retry uses field(default_factory=RetryPolicy) instead of design's literal `retry: RetryPolicy = RetryPolicy()`
reasoning: default_factory is the idiomatic dataclass form for a dataclass-typed default; identical locked defaults, avoids any shared-default ambiguity. RetryPolicy is frozen so behavior is identical either way.
rejected: inline RetryPolicy() default as written in design

decision: Error messages carry no f-string interpolation of request/headers/token; docstrings state status+repo+static-reason rule. RateLimitError/NetworkError doc which ACs surface them.
reasoning: T-I1 token-leak is the #1 risk (AC-2-009); keep the hierarchy structurally token-safe before client.py wires real messages.

decision: Promoted config._REPO_RE -> public REPO_PATTERN; updated _validate_repos to use it. No third regex introduced.
reasoning: ADR-006 single source of truth for owner/name; Collector reuses the same constant (AC-2-014, BR-2-011). Existing 27 config tests still pass.
rejected: keep private _REPO_RE and define a second regex in client.py

decision: _request_with_retry uses range(max_retries+1) so max_retries=3 means 1 initial attempt + 3 retries = 4 GETs; raises RateLimitError/NetworkError on the final attempt; transport errors share the same bounded loop.
reasoning: AC-2-022 bounded (no infinite loop); design pseudocode 'for attempt in 0..max_retries' is inclusive. Final-attempt raise avoids one extra sleep after the last try.
rejected: range(max_retries) which would do only 3 total attempts

decision: Non-numeric Retry-After falls through to computed exponential backoff instead of crashing.
reasoning: Dirty-data tolerance for response headers; GitHub sends integer seconds but defense-in-depth. Still capped by ceiling.

decision: _parse_created helper converts trailing 'Z' to '+00:00' for datetime.fromisoformat (py3.13) to get a tz-aware UTC datetime for the cutoff compare only; RawItem.created_at stays the raw string.
reasoning: BR-2-010 never reformat created_at; cutoff compare needs tz-aware (handoff §3). Items lacking created_at are skipped before the compare (dirty guard).

decision: Added an explicit '.'/'..' path-segment guard in _validate_repo on top of REPO_PATTERN.match.
reasoning: DEVIATION: the shared REPO_PATTERN (ADR-006) permits '.' so '../x' matches; httpx normalizes /repos/../x/issues -> /x/issues, the exact SSRF-shaped traversal AC-2-014/T-T2 must block. Kept the shared regex untouched (architect-owned) and added defense-in-depth in the collector where the spec requires pre-request rejection. WHY minor: does not change shared config.py behavior, satisfies AC-2-014 in the collector as designed.
rejected: tighten shared REPO_PATTERN (changes config.py validation contract, architect-owned)
leave the gap and weaken the test (violates AC-2-014/test 7.7 which lists ../x)

decision: Tests use httpx.MockTransport with handler closures + a sleep-capturing list; no respx, no real API, no real wait (ADR-005). Every it/test name+docstring carries its AC-ID(s).
reasoning: Stack rule mock GitHub, no new dep; sleep injection makes retry tests instant and assertable (AC-2-022/026).

decision: _parse_created() called with created_at value that passed None-check but may not be a string (e.g. int from malformed JSON); .replace('Z','+00:00') raises AttributeError on non-string.
reasoning: RCA: S4 (code) — developer guarded None/missing but not non-string type. Handoff §4 flagged this explicitly. Spec AC-2-010/BR-2-010 say 'guard against missing or null' — non-string is a dirty-data variant not explicitly called out in spec (gap between spec and implementation robustness). Low severity: GitHub API always returns string or null; only exploitable via extreme API misbehavior.

decision: test_auth_failures_fail_fast (403 case) does not assert token absent from the AuthError message, leaving the 403 non-rate-limit fail-fast path unguarded for token leakage.
reasoning: RCA: S4 (code/test gap) — test_token_absent_from_auth_error covers 401 but not the 403 non-rate-limit path. The source code appears correct (same f-string pattern), but the assertion is missing in the test. This is a hollow test [H5] for the 403 branch of AC-2-009.

decision: test_default_client_enables_tls_verification does not assert verify=True on the httpx.Client; it only checks header presence. A mutation changing verify=True to verify=False would NOT be caught.
reasoning: RCA: S4 (test gap / limitation) — httpx does not expose the verify flag post-construction cleanly. Developer noted this in handoff §2 and Known Limitations. The source code explicitly sets verify=True. Assertion is structurally thin [H1] but root cause is an httpx API limitation, not a logic bug. Low severity.
